VWAP Golden Rules:
1) price action often is repelled or attracts price. PA often oscillates around price (good to obtain average price within this range) and / or used to predict or use as a rule to gauge price MOVES out of VWAP
2) If PA is above VWAP, then bullish/long bias, if PA is below VWAP, then bearish or short bias

Sir, Daily or 4 hour S/R(support/resistance) levels, Forex --do these levels deflect price 60% of times?--and if so why not put standing orders at the start of each day in few currencies(each order with take profit & stop loss) and let the statistical edge play out. It may be safer to just work with daily S/R levels but 4 H levels would give many more executions. And if one happens to be watching price action at the level LIVE then combine it with overbought/oversold stochastics/.cci to confirm price deflection chances. Any comments/suggestions?
Hlo sir
No wonder 95% of traders fail.

I find that for intraday forex  trend  trading most important relevant (Guide)trend direction is ON 15 minute, 1h and 4h charts only-- For intraday trading weekly & monthly trends do not matter much(too far removed from intraday field of activity) & can be ignored. For simplicity sake if 15 minute ,1h & 4h trends are in the same direction, you have a trade  set up in the same direction (one could fine tune entry 0n 5 minute charts). In general if daily candle  be in the same direction for any trade it is better but not A must.--Sir, what do you  have to say?

I find the best time frame for day trading is the 15 minute if you want to look at the weekly  there are settings (in VWAP) where you can change it to weekly daily monthly yearly. e.g  if you are looking for a pull back and the trend is up put it on the weekly , monthly better and put orders in around the monthly VWAP or sell if the market is trading down.https://www.tradingview.com/x/DeG01AGS/
